issue_title: Q: Comparison to / inclusion in SciPy?
issue_body:
@choldgraf just pointed me to this repo. I haven't had much time to dig into it, but it looks like a promising method for resampling. Have you by chance looked at how it compares to the polyphase resampling recently added to SciPy [scipy.signal.resample_poly] (http://scipy.github.io/devdocs/generated/scipy.signal.resample_poly.html#scipy.signal.resample_poly)? I imagine this might be faster (argument from authority since it's based on one of Julius O Smith's documents), but from the little bit I read they seem to share some ideas so I'm curious to what extent it is faster.

Any interest in eventually moving the functionality to `scipy.signal` at some point?

issue_title: Reading 2 Graphic readouts at the same time.
issue_body:
I have had this program running for 24 hrs and not a problem.
It is showing local time and UTC time in large Graphics on the screen.
Both Graphics working independently to each other.
No problems with the ram filling up.

I tried  update writing from 200 to 1000, 2000, still did not make any difference.

When I run the same program from two temperature sensors the program runs for five minutes and stops because of memory filling up, maybe.

I have tried the "del" function to clear variables with no success also tried the "gc.collect()" function to clear unwanted data.

For sum reason this program dose not like reading the two temperature sensors or the Raspberry Pi model 2 dose not have the capacity to run a large graphics program and the temperature sensors as well.

I was just thinking now, if I independently assign each temperature sensor with its own independent read out like the program below, it might just work reading two different sensors.

Thanks

``````
import datetime

import pytz

from datetime import datetime, date, time
    
import time

from guizero import App, Text
def update_writing():
          
          
          
          
           T1 = (time.strftime("%H:%M:%S"))

           #T3 = (time.strftime("%H:%M:%S"))
           
           T3 = (datetime.utcnow().strftime("%H:%M:%S"))

           
                  
           writing.set("LOCAL TIME")

           writing1.set(T1)

           writing2.set("UTC")

           writing3.set(T3)
           
           app.after(200, update_writing)

           
           

app = App(height=1000, width=1000,bgcolor="black")





writing = Text(app, text="", color="red",size=50)

writing1 = Text(app, text="T1", color="red",size=50)

writing2 = Text(app, text="", color="red", size=50)

writing3 = Text(app, text="T3", color="red",size=50)





#writing.pack(fill="none", expand=True)

app.after(200, update_writing)

app.display()

``````

from netmiko import ConnectHandler

The recent move to use `__slots__` in `c_ast.py` has broken a fair bit of my code. I pickle ASTs with arbitrary extended attributes, and both of these features now fail. Yes, I can work around them. However, in my opinion, the power of pycparser over something faster and more complete like libclang is the flexibility of Python, which `__slots__` undermines. (If speed is required, pypy executes pycparser about 3 times faster than CPython in my testing.) I think `__slots__` should be optional, if possible.

I forked and attempted to do this but this is hard to do in a way which has minimal impact on your code. I'd be willing to help, but I wanted to speak to you before working on a massive PR which you would understandably reject.

Currently my solution is a nasty monkey patch which I run before using pycparser which iterates through all the classes in c_ast.py, replacing them with clone classes with the same `__dict__` as the original but with the `__slots__` and member attributes removed to restore original behaviour.

Link to Reddit discussion for reference - http://www.reddit.com/r/Python/comments/38alfw/trying_to_define_a_class_which_can_optionally_use/

issue_title: Throwing error when using invalid topic name
issue_body:
The library does not throw any exception or error when using a topic name that is not valid.
The only legal characters in a topic are:
`a-z`, `A-Z`, `0-9`,`.`, `_` and `-`
See: https://github.com/apache/kafka/blob/0.9.0/core/src/main/scala/kafka/common/Topic.scala#L25
This character constraint exists due to [this](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/KAFKA-495).

Maybe it would be a good idea to inform the library user that the topic is not valid. I just spent an hour of debugging because a topic contained a `/`. Therefore, the connection timed out with a `Failed to update metadata after %s secs.` error which does not seem to be related on first sight.

I am going through and improving the performance of my applicaiton including a little bit of looking around at the libraries I'm using.  Since you expressed interest in better performance I thought I'd share this minor tweak I made to allow 'compiled' formats like the `re` module.  https://github.com/altendky/bitstruct/commit/53fd50cf42473bacc49be308eae16536a93d1a1a  It's probably not the cleanest just using the `list` check like that but a little extra work for a `CompiledFormat` class would tidy that up.

issue_title: content-language is the same as the previous request
issue_body:
If you look at https://github.com/evansd/whitenoise/issues/102

You will see there are Content-Language: headers in the response. They correspond to the previous request (for example I made a request to /cs/search/) and now static content served by whitenoise, i.e. /static/js/search.js will have the content-language set to cs. Same if I go visit /fr/search/, now all the static comes back with fr language.

We have the whitenoise middleware first in the middleware settings, and then later we have the i18n url middleware (actually using 'solid_i18n.middleware.SolidLocaleMiddleware' since it let's us have the main url without a lang prefix).

Should the whitenoise middleware go later? Or should the whitenoise middleware clear the content-language header since static content is language agnostic, and would get a better hit rate that way.

issue_title: Example of Object to RDF mappings?
issue_body:
As far as I can see in the documentation, SuRF is more focused on retrieving RDF resources and map them into Python objects, right @cosminbasca?

I guess the opposite use case is also somehow supported: serialize Python object to RDF. I wonder if there is any documentation or source example covering that aspect. What do the Python classes need to provide? For instance I know they need to provide some kind of logic in the `find() methods; so I could continue with a fail-and-error approach, but i guess is not very production.

Thaks @cos

cc @douglaseck, @cghawthorne

The current backend, [`python-midi`](https://github.com/vishnubob/python-midi), does not appear very actively maintained.  The [last release was over 3 years ago](https://github.com/vishnubob/python-midi/releases), despite [63 commits](https://github.com/vishnubob/python-midi/compare/c66e93b529d4c65178c40a72096d26bd903743ac...HEAD) since then. The [current entry in PyPI](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/midi) is not installable (nothing has been uploaded).  There is [another entry in PyPI](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/python-midi), but it states that it is v0.2.4, which does not appear to be an official release, and either way is almost 2 years out of date.  The `python-midi` PyPI package also is not maintained by the actual package owner.   `pretty_midi` depends on commits to `python-midi` which have happened since v0.2.3, so `pretty_midi` is effectively not PIP-installable (not to mention the questionable nature of depending on a PyPI package which is not maintained by the package owner).  [There doesn't seem to be any activity](https://github.com/vishnubob/python-midi/issues/72#issuecomment-247674339) to solve these issues.

Alternatively, [`mido`](https://github.com/olemb/mido) also provides a pure-Python MIDI parser.  It was developed after `python-midi` was created, so using `mido` from the getgo was not an option.  It is much more actively maintained, with frequent releases, quick merging of PRs, etc.  Assuming there are no package-specific issues, we should switch to using `mido`.
Please add a handler for zip files (similar to the tar file handler). Thanks.

from fixedwidth.fixedwidth import FixedWidth

f.line

```python
@itchat.msg_register([TEXT])
def text_reply(msg):
    global QR_MAX_TRIED_COUNT
    QR_MAX_TRIED_COUNT = 0

    def add_editor_qr_callback(uuid, status, qrcode):
        global QR_MAX_TRIED_COUNT
        if QR_MAX_TRIED_COUNT<2:
            pic_file = os.path.join(os.path.split(os.path.realpath(__file__))[0], 'editor_data', msg['FromUserName'] + '.png')
            with open(pic_file, 'wb') as f:
                f.write(qrcode)
            itchat.send('Please scan the QR code to log in.', msg['FromUserName'])
            itchat.send('@img@{}'.format(pic_file), msg['FromUserName'])
        elif QR_MAX_TRIED_COUNT>5 or str(status)=='408':
            itchat.send('Scan timeout, Please request again', msg['FromUserName'])
            //还有问题是我想账号长时间不扫描登录会阻塞，所以我直接exit，我看源代码没有退出的逻辑，这样用法有问题吗？跑的时候是正常的
            exit(0)
        QR_MAX_TRIED_COUNT += 1

    if '机器人' in msg['Text']:
        editor = ZongqunRobot(id=msg['FromUserName'], robot=itchat.new_instance())
        editor.run(add_editor_qr_callback)

class ZongqunRobot(EditorRobot):
    def __init__(self, id, robot):
        EditorRobot.__init__(self, id, robot)

        @robot.msg_register(FRIENDS)
        def add_friend(msg):
            robot.add_friend(**msg['Text'])
            auto_reply = '''hhh'''
            robot.send(auto_reply, msg['RecommendInfo']['UserName'])
            data_dir = os.path.join(os.path.split(os.path.realpath(__file__))[0], 'master_data')
            robot.send('@img@{}'.format(os.path.join(data_dir, choice(EditorRobot.editor_pic))), msg['RecommendInfo']['UserName'])

    def run(self, qr_callback):
        Dir = os.path.join(os.path.split(os.path.realpath(__file__))[0], 'editor_data')
        self.robot.auto_login(hotReload=True, statusStorageDir=os.path.join(Dir, self.id + '.pkl'),

I ran into an issue while trying to handle 301 redirects:

When the prerender server (either self-hosted or on prerender.io) sends a 301 redirection, django-seo-js responds with a 200, it seems to follow redirections.

IMHO this is not the expected behaviour, Google needs to know that the url has changed. I think the simple fix should be to add `allow_redirects=False` in the call to `request.get` :
```
r = self.requests.get(render_url, headers=headers, allow_redirects=False)
```
Is there any scenario where we want redirects to be followed? If so, we can add a global setting, otherwise this should be disabled by default.

issue_title: Script to create a .travis.yml for a project
issue_body:
Maybe use a jinja template to create the `.travis.yml` so users don't have to figure that all out on their own? User will need to define a couple scripts (installation procedure; tests to run) and set a couple variables. Everything else should be automated.

This way users can implement updates to the `.travis.yml` by running this script. Until/unless Travis ever allows include files, this may be the best approach.

issue_title: Parameter that is both required and read-only
issue_body: Let's say I have a spec that has a `POST /entity` endpoint, whose body is `#/definitions/Entity`. `Entity` is a schema that's an object with an `id` key, which is both required and read-only; my intention is that this key is required in responses, but _forbidden_ in requests. This does not seem supported by `pyswagger` — as long as I have the key in the `required` list, it will not validate a _request_ that does not have it, even though `readOnly: true` forbids it in a request.

issue_title: Make "coerce" of fast_real apply to both string and numeric input
issue_body:
Currently, if a string with an int-like float (i.e. `'4.0'`) is given to `fast_real`, it will be returned as an `int` instead of a `float`.  The user has the option to toggle this behavior for numeric input with the `coerce` option.

I will make the `coerce` option apply to both string and numeric input, and change the default to `True`; the default is currently `False`, but since it only applies to numbers currently and the option was only recently added I feel like making it `True` will be the least invasive for backwards-compatibility purposes.
Fixed it, nvm

issue_title: Does not work Jupyter Notebooks (as of recent update)
issue_body:
#### Description

Jupyter notebooks use an OutStream object as stderr, and recently progressbar2 started using stderr.tell() which is not a valid method of OutStream; consequently breaking code.

#### Code

The call that breaks Jupyter happens on line 108 of progressbar/bar.py .

#### Versions

jupyter 1.0.0, jupyter-client 4.3.0, ipython 5.0.0, notebook 4.2.1, python *
Progress bar 3.9.4 (not sure exactly which version introduced the dependence on tell).

issue_title: install requirements doesn't limit the numpy and h5py versions to the max version supported for a given python version in the 0.1.x branch
issue_body: If installing the dependencies on older python versions, setup.py and requirements.txt will pull in versions that no longer support the python version. The files need to be updated to indicate the maximum supported version for the older pythons.

issue_title: Accept iterable of params for args that allow multiple values?
issue_body:
How do you feel about being able to do:

```
rw = RiotWatcher('api-key')
rw.get_match_list(summoner_id=123456,
                  region='na',
                  ranked_queues=('RANKED_SOLO_5x5', 'TEAM_BUILDER_DRAFT_RANKED_5x5'))
# returns match list dict containing both solo queue and dynamic queue matches
```

vs what is now done by:

```
rw = RiotWatcher('api-key')
rw.get_match_list(summoner_id=123456,
                  region='na',
                  ranked_queues='RANKED_SOLO_5x5,TEAM_BUILDER_DRAFT_RANKED_5x5')
```

Right now, using the former, unsupported, syntax results in just the first value being used, which is a bit misleading.

This could break some users' code, and would warrant changing how multiple values are worked with in more of riotwatcher's API than just `get_match_list`, but I think it'd be a good change. Support could be added to preserve the old functionality while enabling the new and then we emit some deprecation warning if, e.g. a comma is detected in a param for an arg that accepts multiple values.

issue_title: Should I fork due to lacking python 3 support and an uncooperatave maintainer?
issue_body:
Python 3 has been around for many years and is now becoming the standard over python 2. All the software I and many others write is python 2 and 3 compatible. I currently depend on python-jsonrpc in several places and need python 3 support.

@gerold-penz has repeatedly stated that he has no interest in supporting python 3.

> Since I don't need Python 3 support (in the next 1 or 2 years), I can not spend time to adapt python-jsonrpc to Python 3.

Has closed several issues regarding python 3 support:
- https://github.com/gerold-penz/python-jsonrpc/issues/28
- https://github.com/gerold-penz/python-jsonrpc/issues/19
- https://github.com/gerold-penz/python-jsonrpc/issues/25

It's all fine and good if he is not willing to support it himself, after all it is free software and he has no responsibility to us. I am very thank full for the work he has done. 

I offered to port and maintain the python 3 code myself and have done almost all of it already https://github.com/gerold-penz/python-jsonrpc/pull/39, however he has also closed this pull request.

This only tells me he has no interest in others users apart from himself and is not willing to let it grow, so that it can be useful to others and have other users contribute.

In the coming days I will look into changing my libs to use another lib or forking this project altogether if nothing about this changes, as a python 2 only lib is not useful in the year 2015.

CC @scls19fr @hapasa @hapasa @femtotrader

issue_title: Inconsistent handling of a username that is `None`
issue_body:
Calling `utils.record_failed_attempt(some_ip, None)` currently creates an entry with key `defender:failed:username:None` in Redis. The count is updated as expected for repeated calls.

The problem is that neither `utils.reset_failed_attempts` or `utils.unblock_username` can be used to clear the counts:
```
def unblock_username(username, pipe=None):
    """ unblock the given Username """
    do_commit = False
    if not pipe:
        pipe = REDIS_SERVER.pipeline()
        do_commit = True
    if username:
        pipe.delete(get_username_attempt_cache_key(username))
        pipe.delete(get_username_blocked_cache_key(username))
        if do_commit:
            pipe.execute()
```
The explicit check for `username` means that neither `delete` call gets made.

IMHO `username=None` should not result in a Redis entry being made. This means that the `utils.record_failed_attempt` function should be modified:
```python

    user_block = False
    if username and not config.DISABLE_USERNAME_LOCKOUT:
        user_count = increment_key(get_username_attempt_cache_key(username))
        # if over the limit, add to block
        if user_count > config.FAILURE_LIMIT:
            block_username(username)
            user_block = True
```
Note the more restrictive `if username and not config.DISABLE_USERNAME_LOCKOUT:` check.
The same would apply for IP addresses, but it is less likely to present itself since IP addresses are typically available in the request.

issue_title: Support status for Django REST framework
issue_body:
This is an informative issue on the current state of [Django REST framework](https://github.com/tomchristie/django-rest-framework).

We are in the process of introducing **official support** for REST framework in hvad. Actually, the process is pretty much complete as we now have full integration support, with thorough testing and documentation. Except this support relies on version 3.1 of REST framework which will be released in March.

This means that for now, if you want to use REST framework with django-hvad, you have 3 options:

- Jump straight to March: use REST framework [from git](https://github.com/tomchristie/django-rest-framework/) together with hvad [`feature/restframework`](https://github.com/spectras/django-hvad/tree/feature/restframework) branch. That branch will be maintained until March, at which point it will be merged into `master`. You can follow #220 to get updates on its status.
- Stick to REST framework 2, and use the glue code from [issue #211](https://github.com/KristianOellegaard/django-hvad/issues/211#issuecomment-62233142). It will be mostly compatible with upcoming official support, though it has much less features.
- Customize the interaction by yourself. Take the glue code from above point as a sample, it is short and straightforward. Open issues here if you need, we'll be glad to help.

Maybe the reasons for waiting until March deserve some explanation. We have long wanted to add out-of-the-box support for REST framework. We knew however that REST framework version 3 would have a lot of changes in the parts hvad would interact with, so we postponed working on full integration until we had a clearer view.

Now REST framework 3 is out, it has many improvements, but it also lost the main API entry points that are required for hvad to integrate correctly. We got in touch with the lead developer of REST framework, who promptly did an amazing work on adding clean extension points. Everything will be wrapped up and included in release 3.1 of REST framework.

**TL;DR:** we had to choose between hacky support right now, or clean, fully-featured support starting from March. We went for the latter, especially since it is easy to get things going with [glue code](https://github.com/KristianOellegaard/django-hvad/issues/211#issuecomment-62233142) in the meantime.

issue_title: Socket.IO disconnection packets are parsed incorrectly
issue_body:
I'm not sure whether this issue belongs to here or to `python-socketio`, but I think it's more appropriate here.

I'm using Socket.IO 1.3.7 on the client side to connect to a Socket.IO server based on Flask-SocketIO 1.0b3 and eventlet. When I call the `disconnect()` method of the client-side socket, an Engine.IO packet with the contents `"41"` gets sent; this is basically an ordinary Engine.IO message (type=4) that wraps a Socket.IO close packet (type=1). However, the message body (i.e. the single-character string `"1"`) gets parsed as JSON in `Packet.decode()`, so when the packet reaches the Socket.IO layer, the body is not a string (`"1"`) any more but an integer (`1`). This causes an exception down in `python-socketio` at the following line in `packet.py`:

```
self.packet_type = int(ep[0:1])
```

since `ep` is an integer already and thus it cannot be sliced. I worked around it by parsing the Engine.IO message body as JSON only if it starts with `{` or `[`, but maybe there's a better solution.

Version numbers:

```
eventlet==0.17.4
Flask==0.10.1
Flask-SocketIO==1.0b3
python-engineio==0.7.1
python-socketio==0.6.0
```
Although README.rst claims "Python 2.7" compatibility `async def` and `await` require Python 3.5 afaict.

issue_title: cannot implements read-only baseserializer because get_fields method is required
issue_body:
Hi,

We need to implements a read-only serializer, such as one describe here : http://www.django-rest-framework.org/api-guide/serializers/#read-only-baseserializer-classes.

Unfortunetly, introspectors expect that the method ``get_fields`` **must** be implemented (to then apply **later** settings such as ``omit_parameters`` or ``omit_serializer``. Just check ``rest_framework_swagger.introspectors.discover_parameters``).

Of course, we could have other possibilities to achieve it : 

* implements a model serializer and override create/update methods (but is that really safe?)
* implements a dummy method ``get_fields`` wich returns an empty dictionary (ugly, but this is how we do it for now).

Is there is a better way to achieve it? Or could it be fixed quickly (by just check if that method exists and just returns an empty data dict if not)?

thanks

See:

https://github.com/marcgibbons/django-rest-swagger/blob/cdabd70562129019576673ee3c6aa16308bcc5f7/rest_framework_swagger/introspectors.py#L379-423

https://github.com/marcgibbons/django-rest-swagger/blob/a312104d447f9f125beb5e428350a2a2f5e8ae6f/rest_framework_swagger/docgenerator.py#L275-326

issue_title: DocType is a bit hard to extend.
issue_body:
This is because of how [DocTypeMeta creates an instance of DocType in its \_\_new\_\_ method](https://github.com/sabricot/django-elasticsearch-dsl/blob/7b83947d574702967141a7031a1743f6aeff1b76/django_elasticsearch_dsl/documents.py#L83). Creating an instance of the class inside it's Meta's \_\_new\_\_ should really not be happening.

If I create my own class that extends DocType like below

```
class OwnClass(DocType):
   def __init__(self, *args, **kwargs):
       super(OwnClass, self).__init__(*args, **kwargs)
```

This will raise an error as soon as you try to import this class. The error being that `OwnClass` does not exist. This is because we're referring to `OwnClass` in `super(OwnClass, self)` before it has been created. This also means that because an instance of the class is created before the class has been fully formed, referring to any of the attributes created after [that line in \_\_new\_\_](https://github.com/sabricot/django-elasticsearch-dsl/blob/7b83947d574702967141a7031a1743f6aeff1b76/django_elasticsearch_dsl/documents.py#L85-L107) in your \_\_init\_\_ function will raise an error.

I tried to play with the keywords and it seems that the keywords are not working when used in the

Ok, this is a really weird error and it passes through a few modules.

When I try to POST to auth/convert-token, like so:
```
curl -X POST -d "grant_type=convert_token&client_id=<client_id>&client_secret=<client_secret>&backend=facebook&token=<token>" http://localhost:8000/auth/convert-token
```

I get the error:
`AttributeError: 'generator' object has no attribute 'session'`

I notice that `ConvertTokenView` calls the `OAuthLibMixin` for `create_token_response`:

__(see rest_framework_social_oauth2/views.py line 34)__
```
class ConvertTokenView(CsrfExemptMixin, OAuthLibMixin, View):
    """
    Implements an endpoint to provide access tokens
    The endpoint is used in the following flows:
    * Authorization code
    * Password
    * Client credentials
    """
    server_class = SocialTokenServer
    validator_class = oauth2_settings.OAUTH2_VALIDATOR_CLASS
    oauthlib_backend_class = KeepRequestCore

    def post(self, request, *args, **kwargs):
        url, headers, body, status = self.create_token_response(request)
        response = HttpResponse(content=body, status=status)

        for k, v in headers.items():
            response[k] = v
        return response
```

This leads down a winding path that eventually gets to oauthlib. In the code below, the header value for `'Django-request-object'` goes from being a `WSGIRequest` to a `generator` without any output, the result of the `encode` method here:

__(see oauthlib/common.py line 380)__
`self.headers = CaseInsensitiveDict(encode(headers or {}))`

The problem, then, is that by the time the request gets back to the `SocialTokenGrant` class in your library here:

__(see rest_framework_social_oauth2/oauth2_grants.py line 80)__
```
        # TODO: Find a better way to pass the django request object
        strategy = load_strategy(request=request.headers["Django-request-object"])
```
It's no longer a real request. And, a few calls down the chain in python-social-auth, it blows up:

__(see social/strategies/django_strategy.py line 30__
```
class DjangoStrategy(BaseStrategy):
    DEFAULT_TEMPLATE_STRATEGY = DjangoTemplateStrategy

    def __init__(self, storage, request=None, tpl=None):
        self.request = request
        self.session = request.session if request else {}
        super(DjangoStrategy, self).__init__(storage, tpl)
```

and produces the error:
`AttributeError: 'generator' object has no attribute 'session'`


Any idea how to work around this? Is there another way to get back to the Django request without having to rely on the headers coming through?

issue_title: Ability to incrementally build a block
issue_body:
### What is wrong?

This PR added optimizations to the process of importing blocks https://github.com/ethereum/py-evm/pull/565

The underlying changes to `apply_transaction` are causing problems in eth-tester.  Now, there is no way to leave the chain in a *half* state with some transactions applied to the HEAD block.  This removes the ability to incrementally apply transactions to a block without digging into internals.

### How can it be fixed

Not sure but:

- Need a way for `import_block` to delay building the transaction trie until the end of the block.
- Need a way to do what the previous `apply_transaction` functionality did, executing the transaction and fully updating the pending block with the latest *state*.

* Version: dev
* Python: 3.5
* OS: ubuntu 16.04

issue_title: Container cannot start if etcd does not listen on 127.0.0.1 address
issue_body:
As it is advised, on our hosts etcd listens on only the private network addresses (10.x.x.x:4001), but in this case it is not able to start any container, because in the generated ServiceUnit files there are no `-C $ETCD` option for the `etcdctl` commands, so the image cannot be found.

Like:
```
[Unit]
Description=lambda-unicycle_v2.cmd.1

[Service]
ExecStartPre=/bin/sh -c "IMAGE=$(etcdctl get /deis/registry/host 2>&1):$(etcdctl get /deis/registry/port 2>&1)/lambda-unicycle:v2; docker pull $IMAGE"
ExecStartPre=/bin/sh -c "docker inspect lambda-unicycle_v2.cmd.1 >/dev/null 2>&1 && docker rm -f lambda-unicycle_v2.cmd.1 || true"
ExecStart=/bin/sh -c "IMAGE=$(etcdctl get /deis/registry/host 2>&1):$(etcdctl get /deis/registry/port 2>&1)/lambda-unicycle:v2; port=$(docker inspect -f '{{range $k, $v := .ContainerConfig.ExposedPorts }}{{$k}}{{end}}' $IMAGE | cut -d/ -f1) ; docker run --name lambda-unicycle_v2.cmd.1    -P -e PORT=$port $IMAGE "
ExecStop=/usr/bin/docker stop lambda-unicycle_v2.cmd.1
ExecStop=/usr/bin/docker rm -f lambda-unicycle_v2.cmd.1
TimeoutStartSec=20m
TimeoutStopSec=10
RestartSec=5
Restart=on-failure
```

I guess it is generated via the https://github.com/deis/deis/blob/master/controller/scheduler/fleet.py file.

When using `plot_by_id` a non-descriptive error is raised when identical setpoints are provided. Although it is stupid to try to plot something as a function of `[1, 1, 1]` and `[0, 0, 0]`, we should handle that stupidity properly. And a single point in 2D space ought to work.

### Steps to reproduce

```python
import numpy as np
import qcodes as qc
from qcodes.dataset.measurements import Measurement
from qcodes.dataset.plotting import plot_by_id
from qcodes.tests.instrument_mocks import DummyInstrument

dac = DummyInstrument('dac', gates=['ch1', 'ch2'])
dmm = DummyInstrument('dmm', gates=['v1', 'v2'])

meas = Measurement()
meas.register_parameter(dac.ch1)  # register the first independent parameter
meas.register_parameter(dac.ch2)  # register the second independent parameter
meas.register_parameter(dmm.v1, setpoints=(dac.ch1, dac.ch2))  # now register the dependent oone

with meas.run() as datasaver:
    for _ in range(20):
        datasaver.add_result((dac.ch1, 1),

run_id = datasaver.run_id

ax, cbax = plot_by_id(run_id)

issue_title: Emails using SparkPost templates arrive empty
issue_body:
I use two approaches for sending email:

1. Use django.core.mail.send_mail() and send the email template along with the parameters.
2. Use django.core.mail.EmailMessage() and django.core.mail.get_connection().send_messages() to use an email template in my ESP account.

I have been using Mandrill up until now, and want to switch to SparkPost. I have configured and published templates in both my Mandrill and SparkPost accounts, and my API keys for both services. The slug for a given template on Mandrill is the same as both the name and ID of the equivalent template on SparkPost. I used SparkPost's migration tool so this should be correct.

When I switch the email backend from anymail.backends.mandrill.MandrillBackend to anymail.backends.sparkpost.SparkPostBackend, the emails sent via send_mail() come through fine, but the emails using the templates, sent via send_messages(), arrive with an empty message body, i.e. there is no email. I can send the templates as test emails with dummy data via the SparkPost dashboard just fine.

issue_title: Lazy import IPython [Remove IPython as a required dependency: Part 1]
issue_body:
IPython is only used in two places:

- inspectutils.py: only uses a single function from IPython (IPython.core.oinspect.Inspector().info), so maybe we could just strip that one function out of IPython or write an equivalent function from scratch. I would want permission from the IPython folks before doing the former.
- interact.py: uses IPython for the --interactive flag, but this should really be optional. You'll notice the unused _EmbedCode function in interact.py. (https://github.com/google/python-fire/blob/master/fire/interact.py#L89) The reason that's there is to eventually enable Fire to be used without IPython as a required dependency.

This task is to lazily import IPython in the two places it's used.

--------

This is Part 1 of the plan to remove IPython as a required dependency.
The plan is outlined [here](https://github.com/google/python-fire/issues/7#issuecomment-284266940).

In our setup, connections to the RabbitMQ server seem to be less than 100% reliable. I am not sure why this is, but the retry options seem to have addressed most problems. However, it turns out to be the case that when publishing a message, unless there is a connection problem, AMQP itself does not guarantee that your message has been received *by the AMQP server* after the client's publish call completes. This explains why sometimes, Pulsar's status updates seem to be going in to a void.

Transactions are an AMQP standard and would potentially solve this, but not terribly popular and there was discussion of removing them from RabbitMQ entirely. The RabbitMQ preferred solution for guaranteeing that a message has been received is using [Publisher Confirms](http://www.rabbitmq.com/blog/2011/02/10/introducing-publisher-confirms/). However, in testing this feature, I seem to be able to enable it, but I can't find a way to actually handle the "not acknowledged" messages that I think are being generated (unless they are not - debugging this is infuriatingly complicated)...

Using Pulsar's `PulsarExchange` class, one can publish a message like so:

```
exchange.publish('queue_amqp_test', {'msg':'foo'}, connection_kwargs={'transport_options':{'confirm_publish':True}})
```

Do that in a loop and the rate of delivery will be much slower than without `confirm_publish` set, which I believe suggests that the confirms are being sent. However, when the [queue is killed to generate the nacks](http://rabbitmq.1065348.n5.nabble.com/Testing-lightweight-publisher-confirms-td834.html), the messages just keep going into the void, no exceptions are raised, and, if an `on_return` callback is set on the `Producer` object [as the fine manual suggests might kinda be relevant except maybe not since it's maybe for the *mandatory* or *immediate* arguments which it then later says are not supported anyway](http://kombu.readthedocs.org/en/latest/reference/kombu.html#message-producer), that callback is never fired.

Because of this, and the fact that confirms are a RabbitMQ-specific feature anyway, I think we may need to solve this problem application-side by having Galaxy send messages back to Pulsar acknowledging receipt, like so:

```
+-----------------+
| Pulsar          |
+-----------------+
 |   |   ^   |   ^       
[1] [2]  |  [2]  |       
 |   |  [3]  |  [4] 
 v   v   |   v   |
+-----------------+
| Galaxy          |
+-----------------+

[1]: status update sent but not received
[2]: status update resent after some wait, and received
[3]: acknowledgement of status update sent but not received
[4]: acknowledgement of status update sent and received
```

Only after `[4]` can Pulsar give up on attempting to send the message.

In addition to the changes to Pulsar, Galaxy will need to:

1. Handle the case of duplicate status update messages
2. Perform the same series of operations for sending new job and kill messages

_From @vishalsurana on December 21, 2014 10:54_

Current logging support in streamparse could be more helpful when troubleshooting issues. While the logs are split into files for each bolt/spout, when things go wrong, its not clear as to where in the code a problem occurred. It would be better if the logger could emit the function name and the line number of the code along with the log message. 

However, this cannot be controlled by users as the formatter configuration is hardcoded like so (refer ipc.py):

formatter = logging.Formatter('%(asctime)s - %(name)s - %(levelname)s - %(message)s')

Worse, python logger does not allow us to fetch a logger's handler and set/modify its formatter. Thus, I would suggest that we change the logging format to this:

formatter = logging.Formatter("%(levelname)s %(asctime)s %(module)s:%(lineno)s %(funcName)s %(message)s")

If changing the code as above is not desirable, then the formatter string could become a configurable option. I believe that debugging issues will become significantly easier with this change.

_Copied from original issue: Parsely/streamparse#89_

issue_title: unit tests: create environment handler fixture
issue_body:
Currently the unit tests that depend on the pipeline module to generate the records, are implicitly depending on the test `tests/unit/test_pipelines.py::test_prepare_payload` to set some env variables for them before running (meaning that if you try to run them without running that one first, they will fail).

We should refactor it and add a fixture that properly sets up and cleans up the env variables (we might want mock.patch).

Right now we only show in the changelog/releasenotes the bugs specified with `closes #XXX` but not the ones that have the external repo reference like `addresses anotherorg/anotherrepo#XXX`, we should show those too.

__Current behaviour:__
I am targetting an embedded device (and have limited ability to install software) which is running dropbear (2017.75).  
When I try to use the existing `copy_file` method I get this exception
```Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/lib/python3.4/site-packages/paramiko/sftp_client.py", line 103, in __init__
    server_version = self._send_version()
  File "/usr/lib/python3.4/site-packages/paramiko/sftp.py", line 107, in _send_version
    t, data = self._read_packet()
  File "/usr/lib/python3.4/site-packages/paramiko/sftp.py", line 174, in _read_packet
    x = self._read_all(4)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.4/site-packages/paramiko/sftp.py", line 161, in _read_all
    raise EOFError()
```
When I look at the dropbear logs on the device, I see
```
[1204] Nov 06 11:19:43 User usr1 executing '/usr/libexec/sftp-server'
```
but there is no /usr/libexec/sftp-server on the device and I cannot install one.  

I am able to work around this issue by monkey-patching [scp.py](https://github.com/jbardin/scp.py) into the paramiko client.  
```
def fake_copy_scp(self, local_file, remote_file, recurse=False, sftp=None):
    print("fake_copy(%s,%s)"%(local_file, remote_file))
    scpclient = scp.SCPClient(self.client.get_transport())
    scpclient.put(local_file, remote_file)
    scpclient.close()

# Monkey-patch
#pssh.pssh_client.SSHClient.copy_file = fake_copy_scp
```
which then let my copy succeed
```
greenlets = client.copy_file('file.txt', 'file.txt')
joinall(greenlets, raise_error=True)
```
__Desired behaviour:__ 
The existing sftp copy_file functionality is more functional (recursive copy, create remote folder, etc) than the scp.py copy and is definitely to be preferred when the remote servers can support it, but it would be useful to have the (limited/less performant) scp copy behaviour available for use when the remote servers cannot support it without resorting to monkey-patching.  

For my use-case, the addition of a `copy_file_scp(localfile, remotefile)` method would be sufficient.  Support for globbing and/or recursive-copy etc would be nice-to-haves.

Alternatively, some sort of interface which would allow "plugging-in" scp.py operation should also work
```
client = ParallelSSHClient(hosts)
operation = SCPCopier("localfile", "remotefile")
greenlets = client.execute_operation(operation)
```
__Additional info:__ python=3.4.5, OS=CentOS7.4, parallel-ssh=1.2.0, paramiko=2.1.4, ssh2-python=0.6.0
The scp.py module works with the paramiko client, but would need rework/adapting to work with the ssh2 client

issue_title: "unknown compression record" for some NEXRAD files
issue_body:
Hi,

I'm getting "unknown compression record" errors when reading some NEXRAD level 2 archive files, for example [KDIX19960521_185016.gz](https://noaa-nexrad-level2.s3.amazonaws.com/1996/05/21/KDIX/KDIX19960521_185016.gz). Here is the code to reproduce the bug:

    import pyart
    radar = pyart.io.read('KDIX19960521_185016.gz')

I have traced the problem down to the elif statement in [io/nexrad_level2.py](https://github.com/ARM-DOE/pyart/blob/master/pyart/io/nexrad_level2.py#L141). The compression record for some NEXRAD level 2 files is `b'\t\x80'` instead of `b'\x00\x00'`. Other than the unexpected compression record entry, KDIX19960521_185016.gz seems to be a valid radar scan. A solution that worked for me was to replace the elif statement in [io/nexrad_level2.py](https://github.com/ARM-DOE/pyart/blob/master/pyart/io/nexrad_level2.py#L141) with the following:

    elif compression_record[compression_slice] in {b'\x00\x00', b'\t\x80'}:

issue_title: Interactively specifying domains no longer works in webroot plugin
issue_body:
This may or may not be intended.

If you set `webroot-path` in your config and don't specify your domains, letsencrypt fails with the following error:
```
The webroot plugin is not working; there may be problems with your existing configuration.
The error was: PluginError('--webroot-path must be set',)
```
This is pretty confusing, both because `webroot-path` is set, and also because the code in question actually uses `webroot-map`, which is autogenerated in cli.py if both webroot and domains are specified upon invocation.

Previously this use pattern worked.

These changes were introduced in e1f0fcc and ffe6226e.

issue_title: Plan To Implement: IAM Mixin for resources to provide idempotent policy updates
issue_body:
Before I go ahead and do a bunch of work I wanted feedback on a proposed implementation

Since IAM is a meta-api, we can be sure that all One Platform resource types will implement a common interface for IAM, there is smoothing that can be done client side for this common interface. Namely, properly executing the flow of `getIAMPolicy` -> edit policy -> `setIAMPolicy` with etag is non-trivial, and common between all resources. 

To implement the IAM Mixin a resource would simply provide `_get_iam_policy` and `_set_iam_policy` methods, that the mixin would use to implement `_edit_iam_policy` an idempotent policy update, which would then serve as the basis for `add_role(self, member, role)` and `remove_role(self, member or regex, role)`, the external surface provided by the mixin.

I am using something like this in an internal project and it would hopefully be fast to implement. But wanted to gather as much feedback as possible on this.

Other things that I haven't implemented but could potentially be provided are `query_grantable_rolls` and `test_iam_permissions`. Not sure what the use of these would be but they are included in the IAM API. One possibility would be a `@requires_rolls` decorator for resource functions that would call `test_iam_permissions` before making API calls to prevent 403s but I'm not sure how useful that would be.

issue_title: Core library does not include init in root path
issue_body:
There is clearly a hierarchy in this package, for example "google.cloud.storage" is a particular package that includes the google.cloud core library. But there is no upstream "google" library, and so there is no associated "google/__init__.py" file created, which can lead to a painful debugging situation where simple code like "import google.cloud.storage" fails.

In depth:

This is a corner case -- but allowing for that: the wheel packages for Google Cloud Python are setup to use subfolders, which is relatively unique in Python packaging. So these packages are all underneath the "google/" folder. Unfortunately, there is no root package -- because the Google Cloud Core is not actually a root package, it too is a subfolder.

The consequence is that there is no "google/__init__.py" and there is no package that would own that file as a distributable wheel. This has some unfortunate side effects when attempting to do an import google.cloud.storage or similar when using Spark.

The workaround is simply to touch that file when packaging dependencies.

issue_title: google_accounts_daemon should not change permissions on home directories
issue_body: Following https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/compute-image-packages/pull/385, google_accounts_daemon is setting home directories to mode 0750 when it installs public keys to ~/.ssh/authorized_keys.  This causes problems if users have intentionally set home directories to have different permissions, e.g. world-readable.  This is an issue with [gce-xfstests](https://github.com/tytso/xfstests-bld/blob/master/Documentation/gce-xfstests.md), for example.  Probably google_accounts_daemon should not change the home directory's mode if it already exists.  (Changing the mode of ~/.ssh to 0700 should still be fine, though.)

Discuss the tricky problems the exporter needs to address. They can be addressed by "doing the right thing", documenting the shortcomings, and/or letting the user configure the output they want.

issue_title: MMS PyPi package does not have a long_description
Package with AWS AppSync schema definition leads to deploy failure
issue_body:
**Description:**

When defining an AWS AppSync GraphQL schema in CloudFormation, there is an option to embed the definition (`Definition` property) in the template or load from S3 (`DefinitionS3Location` property). Embedding the definition in a SAM template leads to inclusion of `DefinitionS3Location` in the packaged template, an error condition for the CloudFormation engine.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. Create SAM template with an AppSync GraphQL schema and serverless function as in the example below.
2. Package.
3. Note that resulting template (e.g. packaged.yaml) includes both a `Definition` and `DefinitionS3Location` property in the `AWS::AppSync::GraphQLSchema` resource.
4. Deploy.
5. CloudFormation will error: "Cannot set both the Definition and the DefinitionS3Location."

**Observed result:**

CloudFormation error: "Cannot set both the Definition and the DefinitionS3Location."

**Expected result:**

Successful deployment.

**Example: template.yaml**

``` yaml
---
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Transform: 'AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31'

Resources:
  MyAppSyncApi:
    Type: AWS::AppSync::GraphQLApi
    Properties:
      AuthenticationType: API_KEY
      Name: my-api

  SessionManagerSchema:
    Type: AWS::AppSync::GraphQLSchema
    Description: Session Manager GraphQL schema
    Properties:
      ApiId: !GetAtt MyAppSyncApi.ApiId
      Definition: |
        type User {
          username: String!
        }
       type Query {
         allUsers(nextToken: String): [User]
       }
      schema {
        query: Query
      }

  MyFunction:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
    Properties:
      Handler: index.handler
      Runtime: nodejs8.10
```

We should decide whether we want to add examples to the docstrings of the functions or not.
While (I think) examples are not included in every pandas object, they are in numpy. [numpydoc](https://github.com/numpy/numpy/blob/master/doc/HOWTO_DOCUMENT.rst.txt#docstring-standard) has an *optional* section for examples.

I would like to include examples and also I would like to use doctests.

(see output of `nosetests -v -w pvlib --with-coverage --cover-package=pvlib --with-doctest`).
I once had a discussion with pandas developers (see pydata/pandas#3439) and they didn't see their examples as tests. 
However for pvlib, `doctest` may have an additional benefit for testing purposes and also it tests if our documentation is up to date with the state of the objects we document.

Using MacOs with AppleClang, under Release mode, multiple modules do not to work (Type not found errors) when constructed via CMake.
While we have not yet constructed a minimal working example, we could trace it back, and
as far as we can tell, guarding the -fvisibility=hidden statement in CMakeLists.txt (line 111) resolves the issue:

```
if(NOT APPLE)
       # Default symbol visibility
        target_compile_options(${target_name} PRIVATE "-fvisibility=hidden")
endif()
```

I would happily do some additional experiments if that is of any interest.

To implement #4249, two workarounds were needed.

1. The SDK implementation of these commands was duplicated in the CLI and referenced directly due to a bug in autorest that would not accept a 202 status code.
2. The service implementation sends both Azure-AsyncOperation and Location headers. The Async-Operation header is used for absolutely nothing, but the service wants you to poll on the Location header. msrestazure is designed such that if both are present, Azure-AsyncOperation takes precedence as it is an optional header. Removal of the Azure-AsyncOperation header by the service would remove the need for this workaround. 

 cc/ @lmazuel, @aanandr

### Description

## Login
While users are automatically logged in, the 'az login' command should still be supported, but not default to a device login.  

Currently it does:
```
jason@Azure:~$ az login
To sign in, use a web browser to open the page https://aka.ms/devicelogin and enter the code BN3K7K4R2 to authenticate.

```

The Azure Cloud Shell has automatically logged you in.
Type "az login --force" to log in anyway

Your current subscription is: Azure CLI Demo with TTL = 2 days
Type "az account list" to show other available subscriptions

issue_title: Issues with 'az network dns record-set create'
issue_body:
Issues with 'az network dns record-set create':

1. No description in command (and no description in 'az network dns record-set -h').  Suggest "Create an empty record set in a DNS zone."
2. --zone-name is a mandatory parameter.  People will need to type this a lot.  It needs a short format, I suggest '-z'
3. --type: similarly it would be good to support this as --type -t
4. For --type, you cannot create a SOA record (they're created for you with the zone).  The SOA value is a valid entry in the record type enum, but not supported in this context.  The Help text should reflect this.
5. No support for metadata.  This is a bit like tags on zones (name-value pairs).  Needs to be supported.
6. No support for concurrency.  As with zone create, should support defensive scripting against concurrent operations, e.g. via if-none-match * to prevent overwriting existing record sets.

issue_title: az batch task create --json-file should support task collections
issue_body:
Version: 2.0.41

The docs of [`az batch task create`](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/batch/task?view=azure-cli-latest#az-batch-task-create) say for `--json-file`:

> The file containing the task(s) to create in JSON format, if this parameter is specified, all other parameters are ignored.

Since this is written such that it can be plural, meaning multiple tasks, I assumed it would support the ["Task - Add Collection"](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/batchservice/task/addcollection) schema as well, however this seems not to be the case as it errors out with:
```
Parameter 'TaskAddParameter.id' can not be None.
```

It would be great if this could be supported as it can be very convenient.

issue_title: Collect feedback on how to make debugging integration tests easier.
Investigate converting `--flag` to `--option value` for scripting purposes
issue_body:
PR #1276 exposes what we commonly would expose as a flag as a normal option, requiring true/false values. The rationale is that this is easier for scripting scenarios than conditionally including flags.

This issue is to investigate a way of exposing these parameters that will work as flags for interactive use but can also transparently work as options for scripting purposes.

issue_title: Negotiate/SPNEGO and NTLM requires reusing the context
issue_body:
I encountered a server that will only work with Negotiate\SPNEGO since it doesn't really support Kerberos but only NTLM. But it does support Negotiate\SPNEGO that uses NTLM under the hood still allowing the use of Windows SSO.

But Negotiate\SPNEGO + NTLM doesn't work in requests_kerberos since NTLM involves multiple requests and requests_kerberos recreates the GSS context on the second request instead of reusing it.

This means that PR #89 will only work for servers that support SPNEGO and Kerberos.

The fix for this shouldn't be too diffcult and I have an idea of a quick way to fix this. But for this to have any effect we need PR #89 to be merged first of course.

issue_title: Consider enable pex debug output when Pants debug logging enabled
issue_body: When the user has requested Pants to output debug logging with `-ldebug`, consider whether `PEX_VERBOSE` should be set (or rather the equivalent when invoking pex programmatically) so that pants's invocation of pex for Python targets also outputs debug logging.

issue_title: Supporting Debian Wheezy is costing a lot of dev time
issue_body:
Right now, we run our CI and releases on Debian Wheezy to support older systems.

This has eaten a lot of time recently:
* I spent a bunch of time getting boringssl compiling with very old gcc.
* Yesterday, we stopped being able to install jdk8 because mumble mumble upstream issues (see https://travis-ci.org/pantsbuild/pants/jobs/289159744)

It may also be making our software worse; using a very old gcc means we're missing a lot of optimisations.

We should consider dropping support for this old system, and switch to one of: A newer Debian (Jessie or Stretch), or a newer Centos (6 or 7).

issue_title: Web browsable API does not encode booleans correctly
issue_body:
Sending a boolean as an argument to a route gets encoded as a dictionary -- if you set `validate=True`, you'll always get the response '"message": "InvalidParamsError: <type 'dict'> is not the correct type <type 'bool'> for ...', if you do not set `validate=True`, then every argument is considered "truthy".  

The API works correctly when using curl directly.

The problem can be created by running the app below (Flask 0.10.1, Flask-JSONRPC 0.3), and navigating to http://localhost:5000/api/browse/#/say
```
from flask import Flask                                         
from flask_jsonrpc import JSONRPC                               
                                                                
app = Flask(__name__)                                           
jsonrpc = JSONRPC(app, '/api', enable_web_browsable_api=True)   
                                                                
                                                                
@jsonrpc.method('say(hi=bool) -> String', validate=True)        
def say(hi=False):                                              
    if hi:                                                      
        return "hi"                                             
    return "bye"                                                
                                                                
                                                                
@jsonrpc.method('nsay(n=int, hi=bool) -> String', validate=True)
def nsay(n=5, hi=False):                                        
    if hi:                                                      
        return n * "hi "                                        
    return n * "bye "                                           
                                                                
                                                                
if __name__ == '__main__':                                      
    app.run(host='0.0.0.0', debug=True)       
```

issue_title: the GPOS is interpolated for a different glyph order from what the font is saved in
issue_body:
per comment from @behdad : "the GPOS is interpolated for a different glyph order from what the font is saved in.  Glyph orders cannot just be changed arbitrarily."

per e-mail from JB "Fonts are attempts to create a collection of shapes that are in harmonious order. Although for the end user the construction of the font is invisible and the glyph order does not matter, in the design phase one likes to keep everything in harmonious order too. Encoded characters more or less in Unicode order, and the unencoded glyphs more or less in the order that they make their appearance in GSUB features, and within that following the order of Unicode originals. And obvious in the text sources you try to follow the same order. Some of it is even unavoidable, because the tools that edit or generate lookup data keep the data in glyph order anyway. Nevertheless the text source are not required to be in glyph order. Some last minute edits, or updates and fixes may add elements out of order, and when the text data is translated into binary, it must be sorted by glyph index."

issue_title: File CT download paths are not purged (correctly).
issue_body:
There's an issue with the File CT download paths.  The File contenttype has a plone.namedfile file field and there are a number of ways to get to the contents of the file:

(1) /plone/Imafile
(2) /plone/Imafile/@@download
(3) /plone/Imafile/@@download/file/this%20is%20my%20file%20.docx

Only the view method /plone/Imafile/view shows the page with file info. On this view page link (3) is used. 

The problem is that when you change something in the File Field and you upload a new version of the same file, this URL is never considered by plone.app.caching to be purged. plone.app.caching and z3c.caching are generating the purge paths correctly only for (1).When you change the file in the file field with one with another name the URL (3) will change, so if a visitor goes to the /view page of the File the new version is served, but (1) and (2) are still cached under the old name. moderate/week caching for File/Image content will allow Varnish/Squid to cache. 

A related issue is that URL (1) is used in TinyMCE as soon as you try to link in a page to a File. Allthough if you have set up plone.app.caching correctly, (1) and it's sibling with trailing slash are correctly purged. 

And more complicated: as soon as you update the file field contents with a file with a new title, the Purge event, when we should add it, will only find the new File title to calculate the purge path, the old path is never removed from the proxy and still available as long as its TTL is set. 


Sooo. A lot of background info. 

My take is that we should remove the (3) URL from the file.pt template and only refer to (1). To be sure  we should add IPurgePath/IFile adapter for @@download, @@download/file and @@download/file/$filename$, but the URL (3) format now causes more harm than what it would solve by generating a new URL if you upload a differently named file, which is probably more an edge case than users re-uploading the same file(name) again. 

@thet @pbauer Anything I miss as to while file.pt uses the long @@download/file/$title$ link?

issue_title: curator plugin is not working when I enable http basic auth on elastic search 1.4 curator
support specifying new index name in the RollOver action
issue_body:
we would like to use in curator this feature available in the API : 
https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/elasticsearch/reference/5.3/indices-rollover-index.html#_naming_the_new_index

Hi!  We're re-indexing a 7GB index, and noticed that performance starts out fast then logarithmically degrades over time:

![screen shot 2016-04-26 at 9 57 50 am](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/1916150/14837124/99445104-0bca-11e6-85f5-b0e3df98eed8.png)

We're using `elasticsearch v1.7.3` and `elasticsearch-py v1.9.0`.  

We're following all recommendations for increasing indexing performance, eg:

```
index.refresh_interval: -1
index.store.throttle.type: none
index.translog.flush_threshold_size: 1g
index.number_of_replicas: 0
```

Our cluster is at AWS and is comprised of the following:

* (5) `m4.xlarge` data nodes
* (3) `m3.medium` master nodes
* (1) `m4.large` client node

This cluster should be plenty beefy for indexing a paltry 7GB of data.  The original indexing only took a couple of hours to complete, but this re-indexing has been going for nearly 24 hours and is only 70% done.  And it only seems to be getting slower as time goes on.  At this rate, the re-index will never finish.

We've tried various chunk sizes in the `reindex()` call, but it doesn't seem to affect performance so we're using the default of 500.

The python script is relatively chill, while ES is blasting away at the CPU:

![screen shot 2016-04-26 at 10 25 58 am](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/1916150/14837346/47db5c0c-0bcc-11e6-8fd9-51a716a0fdc4.png)

Any ideas on what would cause this behavior?  And how to get past it?  I'm suspecting that there's an issue with scan/scroll.  It's almost like the client needs to seek through all the previous chunks to get to the next chunk, so everything is getting slower the further it gets.  But that's just a wild guess.

Fixing this is essential for completing our upgrade to ES 2.3, especially since we have indices that are 10x the size of this 7GB index that we will need to be reindexing as well.  Thanks!

We would like to use a Lambda function to copy our WAL segments and basebackups from one AWS region to another for disaster recovery.  Unfortunately this isn't feasible because the maximum size of a basebackup tar partition is ~1GB (technically 1.5GB in the code but I would be amazed if there was ever that much overhead), and such a file takes more than the maximum Lambda timeout (60sec) to copy cross-region.  For example, copying a 812,152,356 byte file from us-east-1 to us-west-2 took 88 seconds.

I took a look through tar_partition.py, and it looks like it tries to aggregate smaller files but makes no effort at splitting larger ones.  How feasible would it be to do so?  Seems like there are two choices.  One, split the input files before placing them in tar files.  That would add the complication of needing to re-assemble after fetching, un-compressing, and un-tar-ing.  There may also be difficulties related to splitting files that postgres is actively writing to.  Two, split the tar partitions themselves, which would add the complication of an additional naming convention to denote split files, plus the need to re-assemble after fetching but before un-compressing and un-tar-ing.  Either way it would be a breaking change, in that older versions of wal-e would not understand this new data layout.

Is there any appetite at all for this kind of feature?

(I realize I could probably re-compile postgres with a smaller data file size limit, but let's pretend that's not an option.)

Currently, we have `Header.descriptors`, a property. This assume that descriptors we cheap to load and we could just get them all quickly. If fetching them involving calling an external services, we want `Header.descriptors_by_stream(stream_name)`.

The name is up for debate but the concept is useful.
Would it be possible to override existing elements of the filter without rewriting from scratch?

we have a version tag for 0.9.0 and v0.11.0, but not v0.10.0

Can we add one please?

issue_title: cannot use symlinks in git on windows
issue_body: if you want people to be able to use your code on windows, please do not commit symlinks to git. there is not good solution for windows users.

issue_title: InvalidSignature verifying register / auth requests on Mac OS X
issue_body:
Documenting for future folks who have this same problem.

The default OS X install of OpenSSL is 0.9.8, which has some issues with the ECDSA functions (see issue #14 ). 

This manifests as Exception: `InvalidSignature` when calling `complete_register`, which is a huge pain in the neck to debug.

You can test if this is likely the reason for InvalidSignature errors with: 

```python
import cryptography.hazmat.backends

multi_backend=cryptography.hazmat.backends.default_backend()
ossl_be=multi_backent._backends[1]
print ossl_be.openssl_version_text()


When installing python-u2flib-server, use 
```sh
env LDFLAGS="-L$(brew --prefix openssl)/lib" CFLAGS="-I$(brew --prefix openssl)/include" python setup.py install
```
Or:
```sh
brew install libffi
brew install hidapi
brew install openssl
pip install cython

env LDFLAGS="-L$(brew --prefix openssl)/lib" CFLAGS="-I$(brew --prefix openssl)/include" pip install python-u2flib-server
```

Maintainers, I haven't verified the whole series of install steps on OS X with a clean install, though I recall that cython (for `chid.pxd` errors) and hidapi were helpful in my testing. Before I burn a few hours writing a PR against the docs for OS X / spend time on test case writing during setup.py to figure out if the openssl version is solid, is there demand for such a thing?

Is there a reason why pipenv is poluting `~` with virtualenvs by default? To me it doesn't make any sense. If the specific virtualenv is associated with specific project than why store it somewhere deep inside your `~` and not in the project's folder?

Hello

Can we imagine a "--user" option for "pipenv install", that would allow a content of a Pipfile/Lock to be installed into the ~/.local directory of the user? Pip provides this option, and is used by pipenv to update itself.
I should not be the "normal" way of using pipenv, but since there is a "--system" that install on the system (requiring sudo permission), a similar "--user" can be useful.

I am looking for this option especially for embedded the application inside a docker image, most of a time it is fine to recreate a virtualenv inside the docker (it is still better than using "--system" even from inside a docker), but sometimes we want the application directly callable by the docker user.

thanks

issue_title: Allow index to be specified as part of install command
issue_body:
I normally install packages using the command line, e.g. `$ pipenv install stripe`. Currently to install packages from another source I have to manually construct their entry for the pipfile, which is prone to mistakes.

It would be nice to be able to do `$pipenv install great_package --index my-index`.

issue_title: Project Metadata in Pipfile (name, version, license, ...)
issue_body:
NOTE: I am including the request here and not sending it to the wiki because it current says that the wiki is not up.

##### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

First of all, I really like how Pipenv simulates much of the functionality of npm / yarn and package.json files that are used in Node.js development. It is really clean, simple, and consistent. One thing that I find frustrating with my current Pipenv setup for developing libraries is having to manage a separate setup.py file for publishing to PyPi.

##### Describe the solution you'd like

As I mentioned above, I think that (like in the Node.js toolchain), metadata about the project should be stored in the Pipfile and initialized by pipenv. Using a command like `pipenv init`, users can input optional values for fields like name, version, license, description, and so on. They can also use `pipenv init -y` to fill in default values or something similar.

In addition, with this information, it would be possible to generate a setup.py file using a command like `pipenv generate setup.py` or something similar. This command would take the metadata and package dependencies and output a `setup.py`. This command can be integrated in a CI toolchain if desired.

##### Describe alternatives you've considered

Of course, the current alternative is to manage a setup.py which contains the metadata about the project. This is the current base method, which in some ways makes more sense, but is also harder to manage.

Benefits:
First of all, a lot of the metadata that I am proposing is only necessary when developing libraries and not applications. So if someone uses pipenv in their application, they may not find this feature useful. Of course, they can choose not to include that information in the Pipfile or skip the fields in `pipenv init`. Also, application developers have no need for a setup.py, so the feature is not necessary for them.

Drawbacks:
For package developers, this setup adds a lot of extra complexity. They have to manually create a `setup.py` file with the metadata and a list of package dependencies. So as they add packages in pipenv, they would have to manually add them in the `setup.py`. 

##### Additional context

In order to include the metadata within the Pipfile, it must be placed in a current section or a new section called info or something. Therefore, the Pipfile specification would also have to changed, or pipenv would not follow the standard. This would definitely be a problem, but I am not sure how important it is to follow the Pipfile standard as it is currently under active design. It also seems like pipenv currently doesn't exactly follow the standard; I don't think Pipfiles should include the 'scripts' section even though pipenv currently supports it.

I would love to help develop this feature and make a PR, but I would like other's opinions before continuing.

issue_title: Path prefix is decoded in wrong encoding
issue_body:
Creating a virtualenv under a root path containing non-ASCII characters fails if the platform's default encoding differs from its filesystem encoding (`sys.getdefaultencoding() != sys.getfilesystemencoding()`).

As an example, my Mac OS X Lion setup has filesystem encoding `utf-8`, default encoding `ascii`. Sure enough, this:

    mkdir ~/Ættartal
    cd ~/Ættartal
    virtualenv FOO

(where that path has the byte representation `'/Users/gthb/quickies/\xc3\x86ttartal/'`) fails thus:

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/Current/bin/virtualenv", line 8, in <module>
    load_entry_point('virtualenv==1.6.4', 'console_scripts', 'virtualenv')()
  File "/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/2.5/lib/python2.5/site-packages/virtualenv.py", line 810, in main
    never_download=options.never_download)
  File "/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/2.5/lib/python2.5/site-packages/virtualenv.py", line 901, in create_environment
    site_packages=site_packages, clear=clear))
  File "/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/2.5/lib/python2.5/site-packages/virtualenv.py", line 1209, in install_python
    proc_stdout = proc_stdout.strip().decode(sys.getdefaultencoding())
UnicodeDecodeError: 'ascii' codec can't decode byte 0xc3 in position 21: ordinal not in range(128)
```

That `sys.getdefaultencoding` should probably be `sys.getfilesystemencoding`, since it's a filesystem path that's being decoded.

issue_title: remove pin on Twisted<=16.1.1, all versions (including current 16.2.0) are ok
issue_body:
A couple of weeks ago, in reponse to #641, setup.py acquired an `extras_require[twisted]` that pins the Twisted version:

```
    "Twisted>=15.5,<=16.1.1"        # MIT license
```

At the time, Twisted-16.1.1 was current, and Twisted trunk had committed a change which broke autobahn pretty badly (https://twistedmatrix.com/trac/ticket/8191 , landed on 29-Mar-2016 in twisted git revision 4f9e36d8ad21f9d73369f4d506bab30f61c2ad71). We were worried that the next version (16.1.2? 16.2.0?) would include the change, breaking the combination of (most-recent-Twisted-release) and (most-recent-Autobahn-release). So the pin froze the acceptable version of Twisted to the current release at that time (16.1.1), in anticipation of a broken release.

On 23-Apr-2016, Twisted reverted that change, in commit d03fee6d464a651e27f86836b15444d5b47bc734. The breaking change never made it into a release, and Twisted-16.2.0 was released on 17-May-2016 with no problems. All versions of Twisted (16.1.0, 16.1.1, 16.2.0) work with autobahn (at least w.r.t. the #641 web.Resource issue).

I'm wondering if autobahn could remove the pin. I have a project that needs both Autobahn and Twisted, and since pip doesn't have a full dependency resolver, running my `pip install magic-wormhole` gets me Twisted-16.2.0 and autobahn-0.14.0, and then my commands fail because the installed versions don't meet the pin requirements (under setuptools-21.2.1):

```
(ve4) % wormhole --version
Traceback (most recent call last):
 ...
"ve4/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 834, in resolve
    raise VersionConflict(dist, req).with_context(dependent_req)
pkg_resources.ContextualVersionConflict: (Twisted 16.2.0 (/Users/warner/stuff/tahoe/magic-wormhole/ve4/lib/python2.7/site-packages), Requirement.parse('Twisted<=16.1.1,>=15.5; extra == "twisted"'), set(['autobahn']))
```

To allow my users to run `pip install magic-wormhole` successfully, I'd need to pin twisted back myself. I'll do that if necessary (I'm trying to make a 1.0 release in time for my pycon presentation, and I want it to be installable by then), but it will make downstream packaging more difficult, and I think it'd be cleaner all around if the autobahn `setup.py` removed the constraint, now that it turned out to be unnecessary.

thanks!
 -Brian
AutobahnPython itself is a pure Python project, source compatible with Py2/3

issue_title: sparse submit and sparse list should not require SSH tunnels
issue_body:
Currently, when you run `sparse submit` or `sparse list`, the streamparse code opens an SSH reverse tunnel to your configured cluster in config.json and then connects to the SSH-forwarded port.

However, there are use cases where Storm is running *directly* on your machine, or running in a Virtualbox VM or Docker container which is port-forwarded at the network level to localhost. In these cases, especially in the Docker case, SSH is not a viable option for communicating with the nimbus. It should, instead, connect directly to the nimbus at the exposed port.

In the case of `sparse submit`, this also means that virtualenv setup steps cannot run.

I am also going to link this issue to every issue that is dealing with SSH problems.

- As py3c nears a 1.0 release, it is setting up to be available as a package on PyPi, at which point there's no need to bundle it.
- Once 1.0 is released, we can drop the Py_UNUSED stuff from _frame.c and _block.c

issue_title: Add a dry-run mode for transfer submission?
issue_body:
#89 in particular got me thinking about this.

When submitting transfer and delete tasks, there's potential with the CLI to produce very elaborate flows whose actual transfer semantics could start to become lost to the scripting user.
It would be nice to provide some kind of dry-run output (which doesn't have to look like our ordinary output) that at least describes the tasks that the user is submitting on stdout.

That way, if I've written my scripts well, I can have something like this:
```shell
#!/bin/bash
...
GLOBUS_CLI_ASYNC_TRANSFER_OPTS="--dry-run --sync-level=checksum"
GLOBUS_CLI_ASYNC_DELETE_OPTS="--dry-run --recursive"
...
```

I'm not at all suggesting that we support these env vars ourselves, but it's a common pattern and plays well into this idea.

I just had an application show an unexpected error because request.method was `''`. A bit of debugging reveals the culprit to be a non-HTTP request coming in with this first line:

```
GET /info?txtAirPlay&txtRAOP RTSP/1.0
```

When waitress receives that request and tries to parse it calls waitress.parse.crack_first_line. That sees the line does not claim to be HTTP and aborts:

```
>>> from waitress.parser import *
>>> crack_first_line('GET /info?txtAirPlay&txtRAOP RTSP/1.0')
('', '', '')
```

Waitress does proceed to treat this as a normal HTTP request and sends it down the WSGI stack. I'm not sure what the best behaviour is. I can see a few options:

1. Keep the current behaviour.
2. Abort processing for non-HTTP requests
3. Try to be less strict when parsing the first line and treat this as a GET request, with protocol set to RTSP/1.0 (does the wsgi environ have a protocol field?)

Docker Builds fail when being executed in parallel
issue_body:
When 2 or more Docker Builds are being executed at the same time, the first one will run as expected, however the others will fail with the following error:
```
docker: Error response from daemon: Conflict. The container name "/conan_runner" is already in use by container "[...]". You have to remove (or rename) that container to be able to reuse that name.
See 'docker run --help'.
sha256:[...]
Error response from daemon: You cannot remove a running container [...]. Stop the container before attempting removal or force remove
```
A common situation where this behaviour could occur, would be when a CI-Server starts multiple Jobs in parallel (e.g. when building multiple libs with the same docker image).

From what I can tell, the error seems to be caused by the run-method from the DockerTestPackageRunner, which seems to update the docker image (I think?): https://github.com/conan-io/conan-package-tools/blob/a577cb65ae486c0f8330d2b1f09d9698e17eb9e3/conan/create_runner.py#L127
If this part is indeed the issue, would it be an acceptable solution to provide another environment variable (e.g. DOCKER_IMAGE_UPGRADE=[True,False]) in order to check whether or not to do the pull step? This would also be useful, when working with preconfigured/prebuilt docker images which use specific conan versions.

issue_title: Sometimes Patroni fails to drop replication slot and it causes xlog to fill up
issue_body:
We already observed it several times on production.
I think it happens because we are removing only non-active replication slots: https://github.com/zalando/patroni/blob/master/patroni/postgresql.py#L1184

Default value of [wal_sender_timeout](https://www.postgresql.org/docs/9.6/static/runtime-config-replication.html) is 60 seconds while we are trying to remove replication slot after 30 seconds (ttl=30). That means postgres might think that slot is still active and does nothing. Patroni at the same time thinks that it was removed successfully.
Include detailed information with extra parameter when logging.
